name: The empty tomb
notes: The first morning of the resurrection, told in all four Gospels. The shared core lines up closely: the first day of the week, very early; the stone already rolled away; the announcement "he is not here: for he is risen"; the women sent to tell the disciples. Around that core each writer keeps his own details — the women named differ (Matthew: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary; Mark adds Salome; Luke adds Joanna and others; John follows Mary Magdalene, whose "we know not" implies companions), and the messengers are described as one (an angel, a young man in white) or two (two men in shining garments, two angels in white). These are complementary vantages on one morning, and where they differ the difference is left as a matter for further study. Luke and John alone follow Peter running to the tomb to see the linen clothes; John alone records Mary meeting the risen Lord (20:11-18), left unlinked.
